source_pr.title: store/tikv: make TestCoprocessorStreamRecvTimeout more stable
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #16958 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

When the unit test runs parallelly with others, the table schema `t` may be changed.


How it Works:

Rename `t` to `cop_stream_timeout` to get rid of other tests' affect.



### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- <!-- Please write a release note here to describe the change you made when it is released to the users of TiDB. If your PR doesn't involve any change to TiDB(like test enhancements, RFC proposals...), you can write ``. --> No release note

root_cause_explanation: TestCoprocessorStreamRecvTimeout was flaky because it used a generic table name (`t`) in the shared `test` database; when tests run in parallel, other tests may create/alter/drop the same table name and interfere with this test. The fix renames the table to a dedicated name (`cop_stream_timeout`) to avoid cross-test collisions on shared schema objects.
fix_pattern: Use a dedicated table name (cop_stream_timeout) instead of the generic `t` to avoid cross-test schema collisions under parallel execution.

evidence: evidence: PR title: make TestCoprocessorStreamRecvTimeout stable.
evidence: diff renames the test table from generic 't' to 'cop_stream_timeout' to avoid cross-test name collision (store/tikv/sql_fail_test.go).
